globalNotice: Site-wide reference · not tied to a book
ovLead: The analytical lens behind StorySphere: every judgement about a character, event, or symbol comes from a method that is grounded in the literature, traceable, and confidence-tagged. This is its documentation and education centre.
crossSoonBody: As the library grows, individual judgements aggregate into cross-book patterns. This tab will let you pick an archetype or type and see which books — and which characters or events within them — embody it, with both list and matrix views.
noCross: This method describes an analysis process rather than a cross-book classification, so it has no cross-book lookup.
confIntro: Every judgement carries a 0–1 confidence value, mapped to one of three epistemic tiers.
honestTitle: What the confidence value is — and is not
honestBody: The confidence value reflects how sure the model is about a single judgement. It is the LLM's own assessment made during inference — not a deterministic, line-by-line auditable formula. It is shaped by how explicit the textual evidence is, passage length, and cross-chapter consistency, but it remains a probabilistic judgement. Treat it as a triage priority for human review, not objective truth — low confidence is not proof of error, and high confidence is no guarantee of correctness.
noConfidenceTitle: This method does not produce a confidence value
noConfidenceBody: The backend output object for this method has no confidence field. Judgement quality is reflected by the LLM's reasoning string (justifying both classification choices) and the HITL review process; the three-tier mapping below does not apply to this method.

tier.presumedDesc: Reasonable but ambiguous evidence — review suggested.
tier.tentativeDesc: Sparse or conflicting cues — prioritise human reading.

conceptSub.frye_mythos: narrative modes mapped to four seasons
conceptSub.sep_methodology: a state flow from evidence to interpretation

concept.jungCaption: Twelve archetypes on a wheel, grouped into four motivational orientations; the system places a primary/secondary reading on the rim.
concept.fryeCaption: Four mythic modes mapped to the seasons form a narrative cycle; the system characterises a whole book by its primary mythos.
concept.hjCaption: Twelve stages around a ring: the hero leaves the ordinary world, endures the special world, and returns transformed. Chapters are mapped to stages.
concept.bookerCaption: Each of the seven basic plots has its own shape of story; the system identifies a book's overall plot form when synthesising tension.
concept.schmidtCaption: Built on the Greek pantheon, Schmidt pairs eight feminine and eight masculine hero archetypes; with supporting and villain types this totals 45 master characters.
concept.schmidtFemList: Aphrodite · Seductress|Athena · Father's Daughter|Artemis · Amazon|Demeter · Nurturer|Hera · Matriarch|Hestia · Mystic|Persephone · Maiden|Isis · Messiah
concept.schmidtMascList: Apollo · Businessman|Ares · Protector|Hades · Recluse|Hermes · Fool|Dionysus · Woman's Man|Poseidon · Artist|Zeus · King|Osiris · Messiah
concept.sepCaption: SEP first aggregates evidence purely from text in the data layer, then hands it to the interpretation layer; human review can send it back, forming a feedback loop.
